semantic_text:
**Updated as of 4/16/25**

The authors of The Well just released `1.1.0`, which, among other things, integrates the changes necessary to package full statistics files along with all downloaded data. I've updated the API in accordance with those changes and added a mention that users upgrade on pip to `the_well==1.1.0`.

Also packaged along with this PR is the autoregressive eval mode for our `Trainer` necessary to use an FNOnD on nD temporal trajectories and evaluate its performance over autoregressive rollouts. Might be worth breaking this out into two PRs, but the functionality is linked since this is our first public test of data with this use case.
add deprecation notice to README
merge main, update interface, note 1.1.0 release
add welldataset base path

Fixing the wandb name in the training scripts so they work when using  `log: bool = True` in `config.wandb.py`

However, currently the codes don't work with `log_output: bool = True`

There is some issue with the code for logging the image outputs in wandb

```
 File "/......./neuraloperator/neuralop/training/trainer.py", line 433, in evaluate
    errors[f"{log_prefix}_outputs"] = wandb.Image(outs)
                                      ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File "/......./TempEnv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/wandb/sdk/data_types/image.py", line 266, in __init__
    self._initialize_from_data(data_or_path, mode, file_type, normalize)
  File "......./TempEnv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/wandb/sdk/data_types/image.py", line 405, in _initialize_from_data
    mode = mode or self.guess_mode(data, file_type)
                   ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File "/......./TempEnv/lib/python3.11/site-packages/wandb/sdk/data_types/image.py", line 603, in guess_mode
    raise ValueError(
ValueError: Un-supported shape for image conversion [16, 1, 17, 16]
```

Only override load_state_dict and state_dict so our objects are compatible with regular PyTorch saving/loading out of the box. I would argue we should remove load/save_checkpoint completely and always use `torch.save(model.state_dict())` or `Model.from_checkpoint(...)` if needed.
update for multistep input
infer max_steps better
check in well dataset with stats

Main changes:

* Add `transformer_no.py` under `models`, which is a Transformer encoder-decoder architecture for learning operator.  Small changes to the `__init__.py` and `model_dispatcher.py` accordingly.
* Add Gaussian Fourier feature and Siren to the `embeddings.py` which is useful for building the neural field (query point embedding) in the Transformer decoder.
* Add a training example `train_darcy_transformer.py` under `scripts`, the corresponding config yaml file is `darcy_transformer_config.yaml`.
* Fixed a bug in previous attention layer test [#290](https://github.com/neuraloperator/neuraloperator/issues/290)

It is a draft PR that adds the DPOT model architecture from [1]. It is modified and improved from AFNO [2]. It adds two files in ```layers``` folder, i.e., ```afno_block.py``` and ```cno_block.py```.  The DPOT model is updated in ```models/dpot.py```.  

** PR changes**
1. add ```layers/afno_block.py```, ```layers/cno_block.py```, ```models/dpot.py```
2.  add a line in ```models/__init__.py```


**References**
1. DPOT: Auto-Regressive Denoising Operator Transformer for Large-Scale PDE Pre-Training (https://arxiv.org/abs/2403.03542)
3. Adaptive Fourier Neural Operators: Efficient Token Mixers for Transformers (https://arxiv.org/abs/2111.13587)
better sample counting in autoreg eval
